SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.core.key

Class FailoverKeyGenerator

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.core.key.FailoverKeyGenerator
All Implemented Interfaces:
KeyGenerator
public class FailoverKeyGenerator extends Object implements KeyGenerator
Primary KeyGenerator that orchestrates key generation for a failover operation.

Resolution order:

  1. If Failover.keyGenerator() is empty, delegates to the injected defaultKeyGenerator.
  2. Otherwise, looks up a KeyGenerator by that name via KeyGeneratorLookup. Throws KeyGeneratorNotFoundException if no match is found (neither by qualifier nor by bean name).

The final key is a deterministic type-3 UUID derived from FailoverNameResolver.effectiveName(failover) + ":" + rawKey encoded as UTF-8 bytes. effectiveName is Failover.domain() when non-blank, otherwise Failover.name(). Hashing normalises key length and prevents store-column overflow regardless of argument size.

Author:
Anand Manissery
See Also:
  • DefaultKeyGenerator
  • KeyGeneratorLookup

  • Method Details

    • key

      public String key(Failover failover, List<Object> args)
      Resolves the appropriate KeyGenerator and returns the final UUID key.

      If Failover.keyGenerator() is empty, delegates to the injected defaultKeyGenerator. Otherwise looks up the named generator via KeyGeneratorLookup. In either case the raw key is hashed into a deterministic type-3 UUID prefixed with failover.name().

      Specified by:
      key in interface KeyGenerator
      Parameters:
      failover - annotation metadata for the intercepted method
      args - resolved method arguments
      Returns:
      deterministic type-3 UUID string uniquely identifying this call
      Throws:
      KeyGeneratorNotFoundException - if failover.keyGenerator() is non-empty but no matching bean is found (neither by qualifier nor by bean name)
